source.goal: Optimize project understanding for AI retrieval, task routing, and implementation planning.

onboarding.first_pass_rule: Use PROJECT_MAP.json for the first repo scan. Use README.json after the route is clear and deeper runtime detail is needed.

normalization_rules: Commands use argv arrays when they can be executed directly without shell parsing.
Paths are repository-relative unless a runtime path is inherently external.
Legacy README prose is collapsed into normalized fields instead of being copied verbatim.
Sections are organized by decision usefulness for AI agents: what the app is, how it runs, where data lives, where to edit.

project.summary: A local writing workroom where Rune drafts, Canon reviews continuity and structure, Echo reacts like a reader, and Rune produces a revised final text.
project.persistence_model: All state, history, cost records, and logs are stored locally as JSON.

glossary.responsibility: Reviews continuity, structure, future risk, and next actions.
Leaves emotional reaction, immersion, and disengagement comments.
Handles greetings, absence logging, recovery, and error traces.
Focuses on the currently selected loop and its outputs.
Searches, filters, compares, and exports accumulated history.

what_it_is: A loop-driven writing assistant rather than a one-shot generator.
A local-first workroom that preserves structured review data for later comparison.
A project that can run end-to-end even without a live Gemini API key.
what_it_is_not: Not a tool optimized only for producing a final answer in one pass.
Not a cloud-first app with remote persistence as the default assumption.
design_goals: Stabilize the draft -> review -> revision -> next-loop decision cycle.
Preserve review output as reusable structured data instead of disposable prose.
Reduce the gap between development mode and user mode with mock support.

mock.purpose: Allow full-flow verification without live API access.

sequence: User enters title, plot, settings, previous context, and loop config.
Canon and Echo review the draft, usually in parallel.
Rune creates the final text using draft, feedback, and comment.
Quality is evaluated to decide whether the run should stop early.
A history JSON artifact is written for the completed loop.

quality_gate.description: Quality is computed after final generation from Canon and Echo outputs. If early stop is enabled and quality.should_stop is true, remaining loops are skipped.

ui_surfaces.name: title_screen
ui_surfaces.purpose: Opening transition before entering the main workspace.

ui_surfaces.name: main
ui_surfaces.purpose: Chat-centered workspace for settings, plot input, estimation, and run control.

ui_surfaces.name: workroom
ui_surfaces.purpose: Focused loop inspection for current or selected iteration.

ui_surfaces.name: archive
ui_surfaces.purpose: History search, filtering, detail view, and export.

api_surface.purpose: Return greeting, settings, loop state, Sil log, history, meta, and service status.
Estimate token and cost usage for the configured run.

exe_launch.notes: Packaged version opens the browser automatically and stores data under SleepingArchiveData/storage.

environment_variables.name: SLEEPING_ARCHIVE_HOME
environment_variables.description: Overrides the application home directory used for storage.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_API_KEY
environment_variables.description: If unset, the app falls back to mock mode.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_MODEL
environment_variables.description: Gemini model name for live mode.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_API_BASE_URL
environment_variables.description: Base URL for Gemini API requests.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_MAX_RETRIES
environment_variables.description: Maximum number of Gemini retry attempts.

environment_variables.name: DEFAULT_MAX_LOOPS
environment_variables.description: Default max loop count used in config.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_INPUT_COST_PER_1M
environment_variables.description: Input pricing for the standard tier.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_INPUT_COST_PER_1M_LARGE
environment_variables.description: Input pricing for the large tier.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_OUTPUT_COST_PER_1M
environment_variables.description: Output pricing for the standard tier.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_OUTPUT_COST_PER_1M_LARGE
environment_variables.description: Output pricing for the large tier.

environment_variables.name: GEMINI_PRICING_THRESHOLD_TOKENS
environment_variables.description: Threshold used to switch pricing tiers.

app_home_resolution: If SLEEPING_ARCHIVE_HOME is set, use it.
If running as a frozen executable, use <exe dir>/SleepingArchiveData.

non_canonical_repo_artifacts.reason: Package-local JSON artifacts may exist, but they are not the active runtime store.
Same as above; do not confuse this with the canonical source-mode history directory.

prompt_templates.versioning_rule: When prompt wording or response structure changes, bump the template version.

code_map.role: Quick-start routing map for first-contact AI agents.
FastAPI entrypoint and API surface.
Pydantic request models for settings, previous context, cost estimate, and run requests.
Runtime directories, defaults, and environment-based config loading.
Local JSON persistence, visit metadata, history listing, and filename generation.
Main run orchestration, cancellation, interruption recovery, and history persistence.
Loop quality evaluation used for early stopping.
Prompt templates, versions, and prompt request builders.
Gemini client implementation and mode handling.
Sil logging and maintainer-side system messages.
Checklist validation and markdown rendering from structured checklist data.
Folder-level note that clarifies why backend/storage is code, not the canonical runtime storage root.
Operator-facing manifest that explains checklist folder purpose, source-of-truth files, and maintenance rules.
Main HTML shell.
Frontend styling.
Frontend state, polling, UI rendering, export, and interaction logic.
CLI entrypoint for checklist markdown generation and consistency checks.
Convenience launcher for local execution.
Build helper for the packaged executable.
Executable packaging spec.

common_task_entrypoints.task: Change API response schema or route behavior
Change loop execution order, cancellation, or recovery behavior
Change persona prompt wording or prompt version metadata
Change Gemini configuration or pricing defaults
Change frontend interactions, view switching, export, or attachment behavior
Change checklist source data or rendering
Change local persistence format or storage path behavior

constraints: State and history are intentionally local-first JSON files.
CHECKLIST.md is generated output and should not be edited directly.
Packaged executable and source mode use different storage roots.
Loop cancellation and interrupted-run recovery are core behaviors, not edge cases.
History export supports both markdown and JSON at the UI layer.

current_status.phase: Beyond MVP; actively polishing real usability.
strategic_assessment: The project is aligned with its original goal of making loops easier to read, compare, and continue.
Future effort should prioritize writing support quality over presentation flourishes.

next_focus.summary: Expose loop quality scores and improvement deltas more clearly in the UI.
next_focus.why: Quality data already exists but trend visibility is still weak.

next_focus.summary: Feed repeated Canon and Echo issues back into prompt correction or summaries.
next_focus.why: Recurring weaknesses should become reusable guidance instead of repeated manual diagnosis.

next_focus.summary: Strengthen per-work context storage and automatic summaries.
next_focus.why: Longer-term usability depends on preserving recent text, summaries, and fixed notes by work.
The packaged Windows build is a first-class delivery path.
